reviews.title: Amazing ear pad replacements for the Logitech G35 headset!
reviews.text:
- Makes my G35 headset look better than when I bought it new thanks to the red accent markings!
- The installation tool was extremely helpful, and thanks for including it.
- Just a bit of warning for others the kit that includes the head pad isn’t mean for the Logitech G35 headset, because it is a different size and it doesn’t have Velcro underneath the head pad. I just ordered the ear pads only, and ended up using the spare two fake leather head pads that came with my G35 headset.
- Other thoughts: Not sure what some people are complaining about, but my ear pads are comfortable and fit my headset well. I’m just going to assume some peoples ears are extremely sensitive than others to certain fabrics. I like the cloth mesh pads compared to the original leather pads for two reasons. The cloth won’t crack or peel off like the leather pads, and the cloth pads are more breathable for the ears (ears won’t get hot or humid).

reviews.title: They are comfortable, which is the main thing
reviews.text: I bought these for my G930 since the outside was flaking off of mine a lot. I have been using these for almost 5 months and I have had no issues out of them. They are comfortable, which is the main thing. And since it isn't the fake leather of the original, I dont have a problem with it causing my ears to sweat or stick to it. Installing them were simple as well.

reviews.title: Barely noticeable difference between these pads and the official Logitech pads
reviews.text: The only difference in these pads is that the mesh is slightly rougher than that of the ear pads that come with the Logitech G430 headphones. Otherwise, there is no noticeable difference.

reviews.title: great replacement for my G930
reviews.text: I bought these blue mesh ear pads to replace my original fake leather pads on my Logitech G930 headphones. These went on super easily, took no effort, and are a perfect fit. They also come with a little tool to help slide the pads on but i didn't even need it. I also like the mesh pads because my ears stay a bit cooler than on the original leather-ish ones. These are a great value for the price, and I would definitely recommend them.

reviews.title: Good but scratchy.
reviews.text: They fit perfectly on my Logitech g35s but they are very scratchy.

reviews.title: Not a quality product
reviews.text: I needed these to fit my Sony earbuds which are always smaller than normal earbuds. I've used Comply in the past and loved them but they're expensive. Should have bought them anyway. These are small plastic tubes inserted into blue memory foam. The tubes don't stretch to wrap around the tip of the earbud the way quality products do and the tubes are apparently not glued to the memory foam as they separate when you're trying to install them.

title: Audio Transmitter Receiver TV Computer Speaker Car Adapter 3.5mm Bluetooth 5.0 2-in-1 for Home Sound System/TV/PC/Tablet Headphone General (Black) (Large)
description:
Switch mode:
(1) RX: Receive mode, indicator: blue LED  (2) TX: Transmit mode, indicator light: red LED
RX mode:
Pairing name: KN321, find KN321 in the Bluetooth settings of the phone to pair. The blue light flashes twice a second during the pairing process, and the blue light flashes once every three seconds after the pairing is successful.
how to use:
(1) Plug one end of the audio cable into the audio input of the speaker and the other end into the audio of the KN321.  (2)confirm that it is in RX mode (blue light flash). Find the pairing name in the Bluetooth settings of the phone: KN321 click to pair.
TX mode:
TX mode will automatically search for other Bluetooth receiving devices (such as Bluetooth headset, Bluetooth speaker, Bluetooth receiver, etc.), The red light flashes twice a second during the pairing process, and the red light flashes once every three seconds after the pairing is successful.
How to use:
(1)Plug one end of the audio cable into the audio output of the devices and the other end into the audio of the KN321.  (2)Confirm that it is in TX mode (red light flash). The KN321 will automatically search for Bluetooth headset or Bluetooth speaker pairing.
features: ★[BLUETOOTH TRANSMITTER AND RECEIVER 2IN1] Receiver mode: wirelessly streams audio from your Bluetooth-enabled phone, tablet, or computer to your wired stereo, speakers, or headphones (Only support to be paired with one Bluetooth receiver). Transmitter mode: wirelessly streams audio from your non-Bluetooth TV, home stereo system, or CD player to your Bluetooth headphones or speakers. Switch between transmitter and receiver modes with the simple flick of a switch.
★[NO DRIVER REQUIRED PLUG READY TO USE] No need to download driver, plug and play TV computer or Bluetooth speaker headset.

★[PORTABLE AUDIO-STREAMING POWER] Bluetooth Adapter has got powerful wireless audio streaming capabilities. It features Bluetooth 5.0 chip to ensure high fidelity transmission of Bluetooth audio signal for music streaming to those speakers or home stereo systems that doesn’t have the capability.Slip it into your backpack or laptop bag and take it on-the-go without the slightest effort.

★[WIDE APPLICATION] With the included 3.5mm cable and 3.5mm to 2RCA cable, this Receiver Transmitter adapter can widely be used on your computer, laptop, home stereo system, headphone, smartphone, Mp3 player, CD player, Cars with AUX-in and 3.5mm connector/ Computer Speakers/ Odinary Speakers/ Subwoofers/ Radios/ Multimedia Speakers/ Power Amplifiers/ Echo Walls, etc.

★[QUALITY ASSURANCE AND SERVICE] Provides a 60-day return and 12-month replacement warranty, as well as lifetime accessible technical support. Please contact us if you have any questions. Reply to your message within 12 hours.

reviews.title: Great quality device if you're on a budget
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:e49e95641bb1dc426258e889516d11e0]] Over great product for the price. I can't complain. If you've got the money and would like to go for a more "high end" product there are plenty of options available. However, this one appeals greater to the general public. Most reviews say the same thing: It cuts out if too far away and it has a short battery life. Also if it is being used while charging you'll heard a little buzzing noise. You get what you pay for. It does its job and I can't complain  for the price.

reviews.title: For A/V wireless solution, it is workable but not perfect.
reviews.text:
First, my purpose is to stream audio to wired QC headphones from my AV receiver, as a replacement for my broken analog FM TV sound transmitter/phones. For THIS purpose... I bought a pair of these BT devices, and it works, but the sync between audio and video is - versus analog - perceptible. The streaming generation is less sensitive to this, but AV-philes find it annoying.

It is a problem in all BT, compressed audio, technologies - to some degree. Compressed audio requires buffering, buffering introduces some minimum delay, and the delay can be worse depending upon the nature of the audio.
For many programs (sports, etc) sync isn't a problem, but in close-up talky-talky scenes the audio lag is annoying vs. the FM phones, which have imperceptible lag. For NON-SYNCed applications - this is a non-issue.
I wish Roku had a AV sync setting (+/- ms). This is what BT 5.0 does - it delays the video based on the estimated audio lag. *But that doesn't help here because these devices do NOT control the video stream*.

Otherwise, audio quality of BT vs FM is equivalent. My FM phones were very good (AR, $150) and audio was excellent, so the BT pair has good sound in this application. I have not noted any static others reported, except for a slight hum when the RX is not connected. Generally, the BT audio is equivalent to any other BT audio connection (ie., streaming iPhone to car stereo, etc.).
8/18/2020 Revision: After a power outage, I noticed a VERY bad hum. Removed RX side's power, no change. Removed TX sides power (from USB outlet on cable box), NO HUM. I tried several other USB outlets, each had some hum though with different qualities. Finally, I got out a good high amp USB hub & used it to power the TX side - no hum at all. SO... the transmitting side is VERY sensitive to ripple in its USB power circuitry.

One VERY annoying thing - my devices both power off after 4hrs (6hrs?) regardless of input, even though both TX/RX devices are permanently wired to USB power. It ought to power down only if on battery or unconnected for some time (firmware update perhaps?). Other than this obvious design choice, I have had no connection issues - power on TX, then RX, connects reliably in ~10s. Once connected stays reliably connected UNTIL it powers down.

So the good: Cheap solution to use superior wired headphones. Small & USB powered, so hides away easily on both TX and RX sides. Cheaper & more versatile than quality FM TV transmitter/phone combos.

And the bad: Audio LAGS behind video noticeably, 50-150ms. Also, I haven't tried with multiple BT sources - but there is no UI, no way to select a specific source - I suspect this would be a problem for some.

Overall: For the price, I can't complain and wouldn't spend 5x for good FM TV audio phones.

reviews.title: I'm not keen on the cover material
reviews.text:
While the Arae Electronic Organizer does what it says, its implementation isn't great.

The double layers of this product has one and a half flap of elastic bands to hold bundled up wires, that's 18 spots total. The rest are two large pockets and a small pocket. That's plenty of room to carry most charging accessories that you'd have with you if you were equipped with tablets and phones. For laptops and cameras, you'll need other solutions. Even action camera chargers may have a hard time fitting in this slim product.

It's not a problem that this thing is pretty much made out of nylon from the outside in but the material that makes up the exterior of this product makes it look cheap. It's a rough, synthetic material that just makes it a bit cheap looking. I think cloth or leather would have been a better choice.

In addition, the zipper sticks, making it hard to quickly open this up to retrieve your wires for charging.

reviews.title: Case closed
reviews.text:
It's pretty amazing how much this will hold and still close up properly. I am using it for many cables and some adapters, and I am also keeping those little instruction manuals that come with everything in one of the zipper pouches. You can keep a lot of cables and adapters in this, and it will still zip up and be small enough for travel.
I like this case a lot.

reviews.title: Smaller than expected
reviews.text: Water proof? Not going to try that. This is super slim so you can't really fit a whole lot in. Wires, maybe... but devices, like portable routers, not exactly. Slim portable chargers, sure. Higher capacity batteries, nope. Won't fit anything more than two iPhones thick.

reviews.title: No More Ziplock Bags
reviews.text: This is such a better alternative to tossing cables into a ziplock bag.  The nice bag with zipper allows me to keep everything in one place.  I wished the compartments were a little wider, but it's easy to just use two compartments. Nice quality bag, once I get spares I would just pack these with a spare ready and be ready to go anytime.  Nice organization addition to my travel gear.

reviews.title: Handy little case
reviews.text: Does a good job at cutting down on cable clutter. I took a black Sharpie and identified the various cords by writing abbreviations on the cloth stretch band that secures the cords. It also helps organize miscellaneous adapters and charging units. Not sure how long it will last but for organizing cords and small electronic items it’s a good buy.

reviews.title: Good sound and battery life, touch activated button bad
reviews.text: Overall, this is a good earbud. However, if you hold it in your hand you run the risk of your skin/touch activating the voice commands, it hears a random snippet of what you’re saying and it calls someone from your address book that kinda sorta sounds like what you had said and you have to quickly try to hang up as your phone dials someone you haven’t talked to in 5 years, you whispering “oh f*** nononono” over and over until it hangs up and you pray it didn’t actually connect on their side but it does every damn time and then you’re forced into a conversation of pleasantries. So like...yeah it works well, good range but the touch activated button was a big no from me.

reviews.title: Can wear one at a time. Lasts a long time.

reviews.title: These saved my ears! Literally.
reviews.text: My new Bose 700 headphones kept causing an allergic reaction to my ears and the skin all the way around my ears.  Like blisters, redness, swelling and SEVERE itching.  The first time, I wore them for an afternoon and it took 3 weeks for the allergy to be cleared up.  I then washed the cups of the headphones with a soapy wash rag and rinsed well.  The second time I wore them for just under 2 hours and again had a reaction that took 2 weeks to clear up.  I use another brand of headphones daily to watch TV (that I also purchased from amazon) and have never had a reaction before.  I found these covers and they have completely fixed the reaction issue.  I also ordered another brand of ear cup covers off amazon to try but prefer these.  I'll be ordering another so I'll have extras just in case 😉.

reviews.title: Does what i expect it to do
reviews.text:
I expect it to keep the sweat off the ear pads, and it does this.

But since it wraps over the ear cups, it does not allow the ear cups to rest nicely around your ears.
This means that it affects the headset's noise cancellation a bit.

Also, if you wear it for a prolonged amount of time, it hurts the ears and is less comfortable. I wear glasses and this exacerbates the tension behind my ears.
This is particularly for me with my glasses.

reviews.title: Reasonably comfy, but poor fabric quality and small sound hole.
reviews.text:
06/22/22. Revised rating down to 1-star. The fabric is awful, pills badly and attracts every bit of dust and hair. I stopped using these on Zoom calls because they look disgusting. See photo. This is what yours will look like after a few months.

----------------------

Fits my Yamaha MT5 headphones fine. It may make zero difference in sound, but I feel like the center hole is about half the size it should be. Not sure what they were thinking. I'd like to see to somebody make something similar, with thicker, more breathable fabric, and 1-inch hole.

reviews.title: Very big, but useful.
reviews.text: Very large, I was hoping for a snug fit. These extended further than the actual cushion.  So if you want to charge you will need to take off or just pull off a little. Still useful as I was tired of white peel off all over my skin and clothes. But definitely recommend for a bigger headphone.

reviews.title: There is absolutely no bass, highs screeching high and nothing inbetween
reviews.text: These do not fit well and hard to keep in ear. My much cheaper earphones sound much better and much more bass. The treble is very high frequency but that is all you hear with this. Buy name brand and they would have more honest advertisement and better quality. I didn't expect to good bass for in ear earphones but this has non whatsoever and the high frequency could ruin your hearing. Maybe after few hundred hours it will sound better so will let you know. In the mean time pay a little more but get name brand.

reviews.title: Great sound, but a little bulky for an IEM
reviews.text: There are 6 drivers in here-1 DD and 5 BA, but the housings are pretty big. Definitely deep and will probably stick out of your ears, but sound great (with upgraded cable). For what you are paying, these IEMs are a steal. Sound fantastic. Find the the tips that work for you and upgrade the cable (I bought the 2.5 and 3.5 compatible cables from KBEAR and they are fantastic) and you will get as good of a sound as you will find from your source. Add a quality source (I use a Radsone EarStudio) and enjoy high quality sound on a budget.

reviews.title: Great for outside work
reviews.text:
Was wearing these when taking down Christmas decorations and listening to an audio book.

My neighbor thought it was ignoring him but I just didn’t hear him - these have good noise cancellation. Sound quality was great too.

reviews.title: Solid Value
reviews.text: Regular ear buds don't fit in my ears so I need the "wings." I am very happy with how they perform but they are not as "full range" as more expensive brands.

reviews.title: Good customer service
reviews.text: I had some issues with my first set and the customer service was awesome. I appreciate a company that will backup their products.

reviews.title: Love these
reviews.text: These are comfortable and easy to use. Good sound as well.

reviews.title: Good sound quality
reviews.text:
These are good quality but my left ear is uncomfortable. My ear must be a different shape. I was hoping the battery life would be better, but its ok. I use it to listen to books while i work. Take calls when needed.
Most bluetooth headsets have a delay or echo. These do not. It makes phone calls very clear.
These are the best sound quality of all but my Dewalt headsets.
The battery lasts about 5 to 6 hours with my streaming usage. Probably last much longer with just calls.
My left ear hurts after a couple hours, but ny right ear is fine for all day.
They charge quickly about an hour maybe a little more.
DON'T MISPLACE THE CHARGING CASE they will become worthless. They can't be charged without it.
And it seems if they power off by themselves they have to be reset in the case.
All that being said i probably would buy them again.

reviews.title: Fits my headphones
reviews.text: I was worried this wouldn’t fit my headphones correctly but it does. I bought it so I could hang these in my backpack and find them easy.

reviews.title: Very Nice
reviews.text: Purchase for son to prop his beats
